channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Sexual violence against Palestinians in Israeli prisons is ‘systematic’
item.description: Sexual violence against Palestinians in Israeli prisons is ‘systematic’, says analyst

item.title: Miami host committee assured ICE will not be at World Cup games
item.description: Rodney Barreto has received reassurances from Secretary of State Marco Rubio that ICE would steer clear of World Cup.

item.title: After 18 months of mourning, a Gaza son is found alive in an Israeli prison
item.description: A Gaza family's nightmare ends after discovering their 'martyred' son alive in an Israeli prison.

item.title: Trump says he would not pay $1,000 to watch US at World Cup
item.description: FIFA have come in for criticism for the inflated prices of tickets at World Cup 2026 in US, Canada and Mexico.

item.title: Ukraine may have turned tide of Russian territorial gains, says think tank
item.description: ISW says Moscow's advance is slowing as Ukraine claims Russia's army suffered more than 35,000 casualties in April.

item.title: Rubio meets Pope Leo: Can they heal White House-Vatican rift?
item.description: The State Department says Leo-Rubio meeting underscores 'strong ties', despite Trump’s recent attacks on the pope.

item.title: FBI’s lack of progress on Israeli killing of journalist ‘troubling’: CPJ
item.description: The CPJ says the 'lack of concrete progress' in the FBI investigation represents a failure by the US government.

item.title: US, Iran clash in Hormuz as war escalates: What happened, why it matters
item.description: A ceasefire between the US and Iran has been in place since April 8. Both sides are yet to agree on a peace deal.

item.title: UK identifies new suspected hantavirus case on remote island
item.description: The UK confirms two British cases on board a cruise ship and a suspected infection on the Tristan da Cunha island.

item.title: Yemen fuel price hikes deepen hardship as transport costs rise
item.description: Rising fuel costs in Yemen force drivers to raise fares, as passengers struggle with worsening financial burdens.

item.title: UK’s Labour set for heavy losses in elections as Reform makes early gains
item.description: Nigel Farage's populist party Reform UK has made early gains in local elections with wins in Labour heartlands.

item.title: UK elections – early results and takeaways; will Starmer have to resign?
item.description: Ruling Labour party has suffered early losses with Reform and Greens gaining in local council elections.

item.title: Russia, Ukraine trade fire, blame despite Victory Day ceasefire
item.description: Warring sides accuse each other of violations as attacks continue across front lines.

item.title: Indonesia volcano kills three hikers, two remain unaccounted for
item.description: Mount Dukono erupts on Halmahera island, trapping hikers who went to the area despite it being closed since April 17.

item.title: Amid Ukraine’s daring assaults, Russia scales back Victory Day celebrations
item.description: An important date in the Russian calendar, May 9 will be a slimmed-down affair with less military showcasing.

item.title: World Cup 2026 squad deadlines: Key dates, team lists, final announcements
item.description: From squad submission deadlines to player replacements, Al Jazeera breaks down the key information for the tournament.

item.title: Southeast Asia’s leaders confront fallout from Iran war at ASEAN summit
item.description: Bloc is expected to call for deeper energy cooperation and reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Migration is getting riskier even as progress is made
item.description: The Global Compact is working, but shifting routes are making journeys more dangerous.

item.title: Iran war day 70: US, Iran trade fire in Hormuz amid ceasefire tensions
item.description: Iran accuses US of breaching ceasefire near Hormuz as CENTCOM says forces responded to Iranian attacks.

item.title: Thunder pull away from Lakers in NBA playoffs, while Pistons down Cavs
item.description: Oklahoma City Thunder and Detroit Pistons take 2-0 leads in respective NBA playoff semifinal series.

item.title: ‘A calamity’: Why is a record heatwave sweeping South Asia?
item.description: Countries including India, Pakistan, and Bangladesh have experienced temperatures soaring well above seasonal averages.

item.title: Ex-prisoners recount abuse in Israeli detention centres
item.description: Former Palestinian detainees are speaking out about torture and sexual violence they faced in Israeli detention.

item.title: Trump says ceasefire still in effect, but Iran ‘better sign agreement fast’
item.description: US President Donald Trump says the ceasefire with Iran is still in effect, despite clashes in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Oil prices jump as US, Iran trade fire in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Brent crude rises amid clashes in critical waterway.

item.title: The Palestinian shot dead hours before his son was born
item.description: Nayef Samaro, 26 was shot dead hours before his son was born.
